strategy_summary: The binary is vulnerable to a buffer overflow in the 'vulnerable_function' which reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer. Since NX is enabled, we cannot inject shellcode directly. Instead, we will use ROP (Return-Oriented Programming) to call the 'callsystem' function, which invokes 'system("/bin/sh");'. We already have the offset to the return address (136 bytes).
